mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 7.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates, triggering wage growth and consumer spending acceleration that boosts corporate revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and consumer credit outstanding signals a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate cash flow projections, causing S&P 500 forward returns to decline over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day forward returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample Sharpe ratios.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above 1.0 percent quarterly pace indicates robust capacity utilization and pricing power, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in high INDPRO growth periods are statistically indistinguishable from baseline returns (p > 0.10), or the signal exhibits negative predictive rank correlation over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.5 percent signals an economic contraction that erodes corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5 percent
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 20-day forward returns following GDP growth drops below 1.5 percent are positive in more than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect underlying economic momentum that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day horizon without requiring threshold crossings.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-day forward return distribution conditional on positive PAYEMS changes does not exhibit a statistically significant positive mean excess return (t-stat < 1.65).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the CPI index and the federal funds rate indicate an active inflationary tightening cycle that compresses equity risk premiums and forces multiple contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX levels, or 60-day forward returns in high CPI/high FEDFUNDS regimes are not significantly negative relative to control periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 800 thousand units signals a sharp deceleration in residential investment and household wealth effects, which transmits to broader equity markets and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered episodes across a 10-year walk-forward test, or the predictive information ratio falls below 0.2.
